expectations.text: Identifies that RIM and RIM_TINT in fragment() are silently ignored when light() is defined
expectations.evidence: 'RIM and RIM_TINT ... are silently ignored the moment you define a light() function ... it's simply never being evaluated.'

expectations.text: Implements Fresnel manually inside light() using pow(1.0 - max(0.0, dot(NORMAL, VIEW)), power) added to DIFFUSE_LIGHT with +=
expectations.evidence: fresnel_rim returns intensity * pow(1.0 - max(0.0, dot(n, v)), power); DIFFUSE_LIGHT += ...

expectations.text: Uses += (not =) for DIFFUSE_LIGHT and SPECULAR_LIGHT, explaining accumulation across lights
expectations.evidence: 'Use += never =. light() runs once per light ... are accumulators'.

expectations.text: Includes the Blinn-Phong shadow guard float(dot(NORMAL, LIGHT) > 0.0)
expectations.evidence: s *= float(dot(n, l) > 0.0); // no specular on back-lit faces

expectations.text: Recommends render_mode ambient_light_disabled to prevent ambient washout
expectations.evidence: render_mode ambient_light_disabled; — 'ambient/environment lighting keeps accumulating ... washes it out'.

expectations.text: Identifies that RIM and RIM_TINT in fragment() are silently ignored when light() is defined
expectations.evidence: 'The Fresnel rim isn't broken — it's being ignored ... any RIM/RIM_TINT you write in fragment() silently does nothing.'

expectations.text: Implements Fresnel manually inside light() using pow(1.0 - max(0.0, dot(NORMAL, VIEW)), power) added to DIFFUSE_LIGHT with +=
expectations.evidence: float fresnel = pow(1.0 - max(0.0, dot(NORMAL, VIEW)), rim_power); DIFFUSE_LIGHT += fresnel * ...

expectations.text: Uses += (not =) for DIFFUSE_LIGHT and SPECULAR_LIGHT, explaining accumulation across lights
expectations.evidence: 'light() runs once per light, and DIFFUSE_LIGHT / SPECULAR_LIGHT are accumulators ... use +=, never ='.

expectations.text: Includes the Blinn-Phong shadow guard float(dot(NORMAL, LIGHT) > 0.0)
expectations.evidence: spec *= float(dot(NORMAL, LIGHT) > 0.0); — 'no specular on surfaces facing away from the light'.

expectations.text: Recommends render_mode ambient_light_disabled to prevent ambient washout
expectations.evidence: render_mode ambient_light_disabled; — 'ambient/environment lighting still gets added on top ... washes everything out'.

expectations.text: Specifies node setup: canvas_item shader on a full-rect ColorRect in a CanvasLayer, runtime-only
expectations.evidence: 'canvas_item shader ... on a full-screen ColorRect inside a CanvasLayer'; 'Anchor Preset: Full Rect'; runtime-only.

expectations.text: Maps iResolution.xy to 1.0 / SCREEN_PIXEL_SIZE (not VIEWPORT_SIZE, the spatial equivalent)
expectations.evidence: iResolution.xy -> 1.0 / SCREEN_PIXEL_SIZE; 'not VIEWPORT_SIZE (that's the spatial equivalent)'.

expectations.text: Identifies Y-axis flip and fixes with uv.y = 1.0 - uv.y (Shadertoy Y-up vs Godot Y-down)
expectations.evidence: 'Shadertoy is Y-up ... Godot is Y-down'; uv.y = 1.0 - uv.y;

expectations.text: Declares screen texture hint_screen_texture, repeat_disable, filter_linear with repeat_disable rationale
expectations.evidence: hint_screen_texture, repeat_disable, filter_linear; repeat_disable prevents 'tiling/smear artifact at the borders'.

expectations.text: Notes iMouse has no built-in; pass a custom uniform via set_shader_parameter each frame
expectations.evidence: 'iMouse has no built-in equivalent ... push it every frame from GDScript' with set_shader_parameter.

expectations.text: Specifies node setup: canvas_item shader on a full-rect ColorRect in a CanvasLayer, runtime-only
expectations.evidence: 'full-screen ColorRect'; 'Add a CanvasLayer'; 'Anchor preset to Full Rect'; 'only shows up at runtime'.

expectations.text: Maps iResolution.xy to 1.0 / SCREEN_PIXEL_SIZE (not VIEWPORT_SIZE, the spatial equivalent)
expectations.evidence: iResolution.xy -> 1.0 / SCREEN_PIXEL_SIZE; notes VIEWPORT_SIZE is the spatial equivalent, not used here.

expectations.text: Identifies Y-axis flip and fixes with uv.y = 1.0 - uv.y (Shadertoy Y-up vs Godot Y-down)
expectations.evidence: 'Shadertoy ... bottom-left, Y up. Godot ... top-left, Y down'; uv.y = 1.0 - uv.y;

expectations.text: Declares screen texture hint_screen_texture, repeat_disable, filter_linear with repeat_disable rationale
expectations.evidence: hint_screen_texture, repeat_disable, filter_linear; repeat_disable 'clamps instead of wrapping ... seam artifacts'.

expectations.text: Notes iMouse has no built-in; pass a custom uniform via set_shader_parameter each frame
expectations.evidence: 'iMouse has no built-in Godot equivalent'; custom uniform vec2 mouse_pos fed via set_shader_parameter each frame.

expectations.text: Identifies the bug as failing to update NORMAL after displacing VERTEX; recomputes normals
expectations.evidence: 'lighting looks flat because you never updated NORMAL ... recompute NORMAL to match the new slope'.

expectations.text: Uses textureLod(_HeightMap, UV, 0.0) in vertex(), explaining derivatives are unavailable there
expectations.evidence: textureLod(_HeightMap, UV, 0.0); 'those derivatives don't exist in vertex() ... mip selection is undefined'.

expectations.text: Explains VERTEX in vertex() is object/model space, before MODEL_MATRIX
expectations.evidence: 'VERTEX in vertex() is in object/model space ... runs before the MODEL_MATRIX transform'.

expectations.text: Avoids recommending texture() in vertex() for any purpose
expectations.evidence: 'Never texture() in vertex(), for any purpose'; all reads use textureLod(..., 0.0).

expectations.text: Notes baking normals into a normal map is preferable for production
expectations.evidence: 'For production, bake a normal map instead of reconstructing normals from the derivative'.

expectations.text: Identifies the bug as failing to update NORMAL after displacing VERTEX; recomputes normals
expectations.evidence: 'normals are never updated after you move the vertices ... recompute NORMAL to match the displaced surface'.

expectations.text: Uses textureLod(_HeightMap, UV, 0.0) in vertex(), explaining derivatives are unavailable there
expectations.evidence: textureLod(height_map, uv, 0.0); 'Those derivatives do not exist in the vertex stage, so mip selection is undefined'.

expectations.text: Explains VERTEX in vertex() is object/model space, before MODEL_MATRIX
expectations.evidence: 'VERTEX in vertex() is in object/model space, not world space ... before the MODEL_MATRIX transform'.

expectations.text: Avoids recommending texture() in vertex() for any purpose
expectations.evidence: 'Always use textureLod in vertex(), never texture()'; helper uses textureLod(..., 0.0) throughout.

expectations.text: Notes baking normals into a normal map is preferable for production
expectations.evidence: 'For anything shipping, bake a normal map from the heightmap ... avoids the derivative-approximation error'.

notes: NON-DISCRIMINATING across all three evals: baseline scored 5/5 on every scenario, matching with_skill. The base model already knows the discriminating Godot-shader facts (light() bypasses RIM/RIM_TINT, += light accumulation, Blinn-Phong back-face guard, ambient_light_disabled, ColorRect/CanvasLayer setup, 1.0/SCREEN_PIXEL_SIZE, Shadertoy Y-flip, repeat_disable, iMouse passthrough, textureLod-in-vertex with unavailable derivatives, object-space VERTEX, normal-map baking).

Skill delivered zero measurable benefit on this iteration. Either the expectations are too easy for a strong baseline or these topics are well-covered in the base model; harder/edge-case scenarios would be needed to surface skill value.
